description_long: This radiator features a vertically oriented, corrugated metal body with a deep blue finish, designed for efficient heat dissipation. The top of the radiator is capped with a rectangular wooden cover, adding a contrasting natural element to the otherwise industrial appearance. The corrugations run the full height of the radiator, creating a series of parallel ridges and valleys that maximize surface area for heat transfer. The side views reveal the radiator's slim profile and the way the wooden top overhangs the metal body. There are no visible valves, pipe connections, or control elements on the exterior, suggesting a minimalist design. The overall construction appears robust, with the metal body likely hollow to allow for water or steam circulation.
description: A vertically corrugated blue metal radiator with a rectangular wooden top cover, featuring a slim, minimalist design and no visible valves or controls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full corrugated metal face and the wooden top cover, highlighting the radiator's main heat-dissipating surface.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile and the overhanging wooden top, with the corrugated pattern visible as a series of ridges.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the corrugated metal surface and wooden top, with no visible controls or connections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the wooden top's overhang, mirroring the other side.
